blocks.title: Orders, Stops, Trips and Rate Cards
blocks.lead: Every action you take in Qargo touches one of four core objects. If you can name them, you can navigate anywhere.
blocks.body: Orders capture the commercial promise: the customer, the consignment, the agreed pricing logic, the deadlines. Stops are the physical events: one pickup at one location, one delivery at another, each with a time window. Trips group stops into something a driver can actually execute, with a vehicle and a driver attached. Rate Cards are where customer-specific pricing rules live, feeding the invoicing engine. The flow is linear but not rigid: one order can produce several trips, and one trip can serve many orders.

blocks.title: Who owns what
blocks.intro: Flip each card to see who owns the object and what it captures.

cards.back: Owned by CS. Captures the commercial promise with the customer.
Derived from orders. Captures one pickup or delivery event.
Owned by planners. Groups stops a driver will execute in sequence.
Owned by finance. Turns orders into priced invoice lines.

blocks.title: Match the field to the object
Key takeaways
points: Four objects, each with a clear job.
Remember the order: Orders, Stops, Trips, Rate Cards.
If you can't find a field, you're probably looking at the wrong object.

blocks.title: Quick check
blocks.question: Where does the driver and vehicle information live?
blocks.explanation: The Trip is the execution layer. It carries the driver, the vehicle and the sequence of stops.
